author: y4rr
content:
Phase 1B Phase A complete + GPU dispatch wired for existing svd/cholesky/solve/qr impls.

ADDED to ferrotorch-core::linalg (CPU paths via ferray-linalg 0.3):
- eigh(a) -> (eigenvalues real ascending, eigenvectors)
- eigvalsh(a) -> eigenvalues real ascending
- eig(a) -> (eigenvalues complex [n,2], eigenvectors complex [n,n,2])
- eigvals(a) -> eigenvalues complex [n,2]
- lu(a) -> (P, L, U)
- svdvals(a) -> singular values descending
- lstsq(a, b, rcond) -> (solution, residuals, rank, singular_values)
- matrix_power(a, n) -> A^n
- tensorsolve(a, b) -> x
- tensorinv(a, ind) -> A^-1
- vector_norm(a, ord) — supports L1/L2/Inf/NegInf/P(p)
- slogdet(a) -> (sign, log|det|)
- matrix_rank(a, tol) -> rank as scalar
- cond(a, p) -> condition number
- multi_dot(matrices) -> chained matmul (optimal ordering)
- cross(a, b, dim) -> 3-vector cross product (1-D path; dim ignored, kept for forward compat)
- diagonal(a, offset) -> in-house pure-Rust diagonal (no ferray dep)

That's 17 new functions, taking ferrotorch-core::linalg from 8 -> 25 fns (~75% of audit-flagged gap closed; remaining: ldl_*, householder_product, matrix_exp, solve_triangular, the *_ex status-returning variants).

ALSO WIRED: GPU dispatch for the EXISTING svd/cholesky/solve/qr in ferrotorch-core::linalg. Their cuSOLVER impls were already in ferrotorch-gpu/src/cusolver.rs and ferrotorch-gpu/src/backend_impl.rs but ferrotorch-core was rejecting GPU tensors with require_cpu. Now they dispatch via gpu_backend().svd_f32/etc., returning GPU-resident output tensors. solve also got a DeviceMismatch check for a/b consistency.

NOT YET WIRED: the new linalg ops (eigh/eigvalsh/eig/eigvals/lu/lstsq/etc.) still call require_cpu — their cuSOLVER backends don't exist yet. Filed #575 to add eigh/eig/lu/lstsq cuSOLVER impls. Filed #576 for det/inv/pinv/matrix_norm GPU paths (smaller / lower priority).

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline):
- forbidden patterns grep clean: no .to(Device::Cpu) inside compute fns, no tensor_to_cpu/cpu_to_gpu sandwich
- new GPU paths (svd/cholesky/solve/qr) call gpu_handle() + backend.x_f32() and wrap result in TensorStorage::gpu — no host bounce
- CPU-only paths (eigh/eig/lu/etc.) explicitly require_cpu and return InvalidArgument on GPU tensors — graceful unsupported, not silent fallback
- Existing GPU eigh/eig/lu in cuSOLVER could be added in #575 follow-up; wiring is straightforward when those impls land

Tests: 24 new in linalg::tests covering eigh/eigvalsh reconstruction, eig complex-output shape, lu PLU reconstruction, svdvals descending order, lstsq overdetermined linear regression (verifies slope/intercept/rank), matrix_power identity and squaring, matrix_rank full vs singular, slogdet sign conventions, cond identity, vector_norm L1/L2/Inf, multi_dot chain, cross basis-vector identity, diagonal main + offset positive/negative, tensorinv 2x2 matrix-form.

linalg.rs grew from 703 -> 1430 LOC. lib.rs accesses via crate::linalg::* namespace (mirrors torch.linalg.*); avoids cond/det/inv name collisions with autograd::cond etc.

Workspace: 4499 passed, 0 failed, 99 ignored (was 4475; +24 new linalg tests). Closes #565 (Phase A complete; Phase B GPU work split into #575/#576).
